requirements.title: Zone.String returns unique human-readable name
requirements.text: Zone.String() shall return a unique, non-empty, human-readable string for each defined Zone constant. Two distinct Zone constants shall never return the same string.

requirements.title: ZoneUnknown has value 0
requirements.text: ZoneUnknown shall have the numeric value 0, making it the zero value of the Zone type and a safe default for uninitialized variables.

requirements.title: ZoneFrontLeft has value 1
requirements.text: ZoneFrontLeft shall have the numeric value 1.

requirements.title: ZoneFrontRight has value 2
requirements.text: ZoneFrontRight shall have the numeric value 2.

requirements.title: ZoneRearLeft has value 3
requirements.text: ZoneRearLeft shall have the numeric value 3.

requirements.title: ZoneRearRight has value 4
requirements.text: ZoneRearRight shall have the numeric value 4.

requirements.title: ZoneCentral has value 5
requirements.text: ZoneCentral shall have the numeric value 5.

requirements.title: All named Zone constants are distinct
requirements.text: ZoneFrontLeft, ZoneFrontRight, ZoneRearLeft, ZoneRearRight, and ZoneCentral shall each have a different numeric value from every other named zone constant. Duplicate zone values would cause silent misrouting of commands.

requirements.title: PriorityNormal has value 0
requirements.text: PriorityNormal shall have the numeric value 0, making it the zero value of Priority and the safe default for Command fields left unset.

requirements.title: PriorityHigh is greater than PriorityNormal
requirements.text: The numeric value of PriorityHigh shall be strictly greater than PriorityNormal, enabling implementations to order commands by comparing Priority values directly.

requirements.title: PriorityCritical is greater than PriorityHigh
requirements.text: The numeric value of PriorityCritical shall be strictly greater than PriorityHigh, ensuring safety-critical commands (e.g. CmdWatchdog) can be unconditionally pre-empted above normal and high priority traffic.

requirements.title: CmdNoop has value 0
requirements.text: CmdNoop shall have the numeric value 0, making it the zero value of CommandType. An uninitialized Command.Type shall be interpreted as a safe no-op.

requirements.title: CmdSet has value 1
requirements.text: CmdSet shall have the numeric value 1.

requirements.title: CmdGet has value 2
requirements.text: CmdGet shall have the numeric value 2.

requirements.title: CmdReset has value 3
requirements.text: CmdReset shall have the numeric value 3.

requirements.title: CmdWatchdog has value 4
requirements.text: CmdWatchdog shall have the numeric value 4. The watchdog kick command shall be a stable, named constant so integrators can schedule it reliably.

requirements.title: All CommandType constants are distinct
requirements.text: CmdNoop, CmdSet, CmdGet, CmdReset, and CmdWatchdog shall each have a different numeric value. Duplicate command type values would cause a zone controller to misinterpret a command's intent.

requirements.title: ResponseStatus.String returns unique human-readable name
requirements.text: ResponseStatus.String() shall return a unique, non-empty, human-readable string for each defined ResponseStatus constant.

requirements.title: StatusOK has value 0
requirements.text: StatusOK shall have the numeric value 0, making it the zero value of ResponseStatus. An uninitialized Response.Status shall be interpreted as success.

requirements.title: StatusError has value 1
requirements.text: StatusError shall have the numeric value 1.

requirements.title: StatusTimeout has value 2
requirements.text: StatusTimeout shall have the numeric value 2.

requirements.title: StatusBusy has value 3
requirements.text: StatusBusy shall have the numeric value 3.

requirements.title: All ResponseStatus constants are distinct
requirements.text: StatusOK, StatusError, StatusTimeout, StatusBusy, and StatusUnknown shall each have a different numeric value. Duplicate values would cause a caller to misinterpret a command outcome.

requirements.title: ErrClosed is non-nil
requirements.text: The sentinel error ErrClosed shall be non-nil so that callers can distinguish it from a nil error return.

requirements.title: ErrNotFound is non-nil
requirements.text: The sentinel error ErrNotFound shall be non-nil.

requirements.title: ErrAlreadyExists is non-nil
requirements.text: The sentinel error ErrAlreadyExists shall be non-nil.

requirements.title: ErrTimeout is non-nil
requirements.text: The sentinel error ErrTimeout shall be non-nil.

requirements.title: ErrBusy is non-nil
requirements.text: The sentinel error ErrBusy shall be non-nil.

requirements.title: All sentinel errors are mutually distinct
requirements.text: ErrClosed, ErrNotFound, ErrAlreadyExists, ErrTimeout, and ErrBusy shall all be distinct error values such that errors.Is(errA, errB) returns false for any two different sentinels. Duplicate sentinels would prevent callers from distinguishing error causes.

requirements.title: ErrClosed detectable through wrapping chain
requirements.text: Any error returned by a closed Controller or Registry shall satisfy errors.Is(err, rcp.ErrClosed). Callers must be able to detect closed-entity conditions without parsing error strings.

requirements.title: ErrNotFound detectable through wrapping chain
requirements.text: Any error returned for a missing zone lookup shall satisfy errors.Is(err, rcp.ErrNotFound).

requirements.title: ErrAlreadyExists detectable through wrapping chain
requirements.text: Any error returned for a duplicate zone registration shall satisfy errors.Is(err, rcp.ErrAlreadyExists).

requirements.title: ErrTimeout detectable through wrapping chain
requirements.text: Any error returned due to a context deadline or cancellation shall satisfy errors.Is(err, rcp.ErrTimeout). This allows integrators to distinguish a transport timeout from a logic error.

requirements.title: Zero-value Command is a safe no-op
requirements.text: A zero-value Command (var cmd rcp.Command) shall have Zone=ZoneUnknown, Type=CmdNoop, and Priority=PriorityNormal. No field of a zero-value Command shall require explicit initialisation to avoid undefined behaviour.

requirements.title: Command.Payload may be nil
requirements.text: Command.Payload shall be an optional field; a nil Payload shall be accepted by any Controller.Send implementation without error or panic.

requirements.title: Response.CommandID echoes the originating Command.ID
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return a Response whose CommandID field equals the ID field of the submitted Command. This allows the caller to correlate responses to commands in a pipelined command stream.

requirements.title: Response.Zone identifies the zone that processed the command
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return a Response whose Zone field equals the Zone of the Controller that processed the command. A mismatch would indicate a misrouted command (safety goal SG-002).

requirements.title: Zero-value Response has StatusOK
requirements.text: A zero-value Response (var r rcp.Response) shall have Status=StatusOK (value 0). An uninitialised Response shall not indicate a spurious error.

requirements.title: Status.Zone identifies the publishing controller
requirements.text: Every Status published by a Controller shall carry a Zone field equal to the Controller's declared zone. A missing or wrong Zone would prevent the subscriber from identifying the source of the update.

requirements.title: Status.Seq is monotonically increasing
requirements.text: Each successive Status published by a given Controller instance shall have a Seq value strictly greater than the previous one. A subscriber can detect dropped or reordered updates by tracking Seq.

requirements.title: Status.Healthy is true while the controller is open
requirements.text: A Status published by a Controller that has not yet been closed shall carry Healthy=true.

requirements.title: Status.Payload carries the bytes passed to Publish
requirements.text: Status.Payload in every delivered Status update shall be byte-for-byte identical to the payload argument supplied to the Publish call that produced it.

requirements.title: Status.Payload may be nil
requirements.text: Publish shall accept a nil payload without error or panic. The resulting Status.Payload shall be nil.

requirements.title: Controller Send returns StatusOK with no handler
requirements.text: When no Handler is configured, Controller.Send shall return a non-nil Response with Status=StatusOK.

requirements.title: Controller Send dispatches to the configured handler
requirements.text: When a Handler is configured, Controller.Send shall invoke the handler exactly once per Send call and return the handler's Response verbatim.

requirements.title: Controller Send rejected after Close
requirements.text: Controller.Send called after Controller.Close shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrClosed. No response shall be returned.

requirements.title: Controller Send respects a cancelled context
requirements.text: If the context passed to Controller.Send is already cancelled at call time, Send shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrTimeout without invoking the handler.

requirements.title: Controller Close is idempotent
requirements.text: Controller.Close shall be safe to call multiple times. Every call after the first shall return nil without side effects.

requirements.title: Published Status is delivered to all active subscribers
requirements.text: A Status produced by Publish shall be received by every channel returned by an active Subscribe call on the same controller.

requirements.title: Subscribe channels are closed when the controller closes
requirements.text: When Controller.Close is called, all channels previously returned by Subscribe shall be closed. A subscriber ranging over a closed channel shall not block indefinitely.

requirements.title: Subscribe rejected after Close
requirements.text: Controller.Subscribe called after Controller.Close shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrClosed. No channel shall be returned.

requirements.title: Controller.Zone returns the declared zone
requirements.text: Controller.Zone shall return the Zone value provided at construction time for the lifetime of the controller.

requirements.title: Subscribe Seq values are strictly increasing
requirements.text: Successive Status updates received on a Subscribe channel shall carry strictly increasing Seq values, enabling subscribers to detect gaps in the update stream.

requirements.title: Subscribe channel is closed when its context is cancelled
requirements.text: When the context passed to Subscribe is cancelled, the channel returned by that Subscribe call shall be closed. Other subscriber channels on the same controller shall be unaffected.

requirements.title: Multiple concurrent subscribers each receive published Status
requirements.text: When multiple Subscribe calls are active on the same controller, each returned channel shall independently receive every Status published via Publish.

requirements.title: CmdNoop is accepted without error
requirements.text: Controller.Send with Command.Type=CmdNoop shall return StatusOK and shall not return an error. The zero-value command type must always be safe to send.

requirements.title: CmdWatchdog is accepted without error
requirements.text: Controller.Send with Command.Type=CmdWatchdog shall return StatusOK and shall not return an error (safety goal SG-003: the watchdog kick command must be deliverable).

requirements.title: CmdReset is accepted without error
requirements.text: Controller.Send with Command.Type=CmdReset shall return StatusOK and shall not return an error.

requirements.title: Handler Response is returned verbatim
requirements.text: When a Handler is configured, the Response pointer returned by the handler shall be returned directly by Controller.Send without modification. The caller receives exactly what the handler produced.

requirements.title: Publish on a closed controller does not panic
requirements.text: Calling Publish on a controller after Controller.Close shall not panic. The call shall be a silent no-op; any pre-existing subscriber channels are already closed.

requirements.title: Concurrent Send calls are data-race free
requirements.text: Multiple concurrent calls to Controller.Send on the same controller shall be free of data races as detected by the Go race detector.

requirements.title: Concurrent Publish and Subscribe are data-race free
requirements.text: Concurrent calls to Publish and Subscribe on the same controller shall be free of data races as detected by the Go race detector.

requirements.title: Subscribe channel carries the correct Zone
requirements.text: Every Status received on a Subscribe channel shall carry a Zone field equal to the subscribing Controller's Zone (safety goal SG-002: zone identity must be preserved in all messages).

requirements.title: Subscribe channel carries correct Payload
requirements.text: Status.Payload on each received update shall be byte-for-byte identical to the payload argument of the Publish call that produced it.

requirements.title: Subscribe channel carries Healthy=true for open controller
requirements.text: Status.Healthy shall be true on every update published while the controller is open.

requirements.title: Cancelled context does not invoke handler
requirements.text: If the context passed to Send is already cancelled, the Handler shall not be invoked. A timed-out command must not silently trigger an actuator action.

requirements.title: Send with nil Payload does not panic
requirements.text: Controller.Send with Command.Payload=nil shall not panic. A nil payload is a valid command with no additional data.

requirements.title: NewRegistry pre-populates all five standard zones
requirements.text: A Registry returned by NewRegistry shall contain controllers for ZoneFrontLeft, ZoneFrontRight, ZoneRearLeft, ZoneRearRight, and ZoneCentral, all immediately accessible via Lookup.

requirements.title: Duplicate zone registration returns ErrAlreadyExists
requirements.text: Registry.Register for a zone that already has a registered controller shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrAlreadyExists. The existing controller shall remain registered and unaffected.

requirements.title: Deregister removes the zone and closes its controller
requirements.text: Registry.Deregister shall remove the controller for the given zone from the registry and call Close on it. After deregistration, Lookup for the same zone shall return ErrNotFound.

requirements.title: Lookup returns ErrNotFound for an absent zone
requirements.text: Registry.Lookup for a zone with no registered controller shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrNotFound.

requirements.title: Registry Close is idempotent
requirements.text: Registry.Close shall be safe to call multiple times. Every call after the first shall return nil without side effects.

requirements.title: Controllers returns all currently registered controllers
requirements.text: Registry.Controllers shall return exactly the set of controllers that are currently registered, with no duplicates and no omissions.

requirements.title: Register rejected after Registry Close
requirements.text: Registry.Register called after Registry.Close shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrClosed.

requirements.title: Deregister returns ErrNotFound for a zone that was never registered
requirements.text: Registry.Deregister for a zone that has no registered controller shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrNotFound, without modifying any other registered zone.

requirements.title: Registered controller is immediately retrievable via Lookup
requirements.text: After a successful Registry.Register call, Registry.Lookup for the same zone shall return the registered controller without error.

requirements.title: Registry Close calls Close on all registered controllers
requirements.text: Registry.Close shall call Close on every controller currently registered in the registry before returning. Controllers must not be left open after the registry is closed.

requirements.title: Lookup on a closed Registry returns an error
requirements.text: Registry.Lookup called after Registry.Close shall return a non-nil error. The caller must not receive a controller from a closed registry.

requirements.title: Deregister for an already-deregistered zone returns ErrNotFound
requirements.text: A second call to Registry.Deregister for a zone that was already deregistered shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrNotFound.

requirements.title: ErrZoneMismatch is a distinct non-nil sentinel error
requirements.text: ErrZoneMismatch shall be a non-nil sentinel error that is distinct from all other sentinel errors (ErrClosed, ErrNotFound, ErrAlreadyExists, ErrTimeout, ErrBusy) so that callers can detect zone-misrouting without parsing error strings.

requirements.title: Controller.Send rejects commands whose zone does not match the controller zone
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrZoneMismatch when cmd.Zone does not equal the zone declared by the controller. A misrouted command must never be silently processed (safety goal SG-002).

requirements.title: Controller.Send copies Command.Payload before passing to the handler
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall copy Command.Payload into a new slice before invoking the handler. Subsequent modification of the original slice by the caller shall not affect the payload seen by the handler or any stored reference.

requirements.title: Controller.Publish copies the payload before embedding in Status
requirements.text: Controller.Publish shall copy the payload argument into a new slice before embedding it in the Status value delivered to subscribers. Subsequent modification of the original slice by the caller shall not affect the payload seen by any subscriber.

requirements.title: Registry.Lookup returns ErrClosed when called on a closed registry
requirements.text: Registry.Lookup called after Registry.Close shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrClosed, not ErrNotFound. A caller polling on ErrNotFound must not loop indefinitely after the registry is closed.

requirements.title: Command latency bounded under GC pressure
requirements.text: The go-RCP mock controller shall deliver commands via Send with a maximum observed latency less than half the watchdog period (5 ms at 100 Hz) when measured under sustained 64 MiB/s GC allocation pressure. Evidence shall be recorded in safety/COMMAND_LATENCY.md.

requirements.title: UDP transport encodes and decodes Command frames correctly
requirements.text: The UDP transport shall encode a Command into a 16-byte header plus payload and decode an identical Command at the receiver. All fields (Zone, Type, Priority, ID, Payload) shall round-trip without loss.

requirements.title: UDP Controller routes Response to correct pending Send
requirements.text: The UDP Controller.readLoop shall match incoming Response frames to outstanding Send calls by CommandID and deliver them. Unmatched responses shall be silently discarded.

requirements.title: UDP transport encodes and decodes Response payload correctly
requirements.text: The UDP transport shall encode a Response payload into the wire frame and the receiving Controller shall decode an identical payload. Payload bytes shall survive the round-trip unmodified.

requirements.title: UDP Controller rejects zone-mismatched Send
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return ErrZoneMismatch when cmd.Zone does not match the Controller's zone, consistent with REQ-CTRL-025.

requirements.title: UDP Controller.Send returns ErrTimeout on context expiry
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrTimeout when the provided context is cancelled or its deadline expires before a response is received.

requirements.title: UDP Controller.Send returns ErrClosed after Close
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrClosed when called after Controller.Close has been called.

requirements.title: UDP Subscribe receives Status published by ZoneServer
requirements.text: A Controller that has called Subscribe shall receive Status frames on the returned channel when the ZoneServer calls Publish. The Status Zone field shall match the controller's zone.

requirements.title: UDP ZoneServer fans out Status to all subscribers
requirements.text: When multiple Controllers have subscribed, a single ZoneServer.Publish call shall deliver one Status frame to every subscriber.

requirements.title: UDP Subscribe channel closes on context cancellation
requirements.text: The Status channel returned by Subscribe shall be closed when the subscriber's context is cancelled, allowing callers to range over it safely.

requirements.title: UDP Registry.Dial resolves address and registers controller
requirements.text: Registry.Dial shall resolve the server address, dial a Controller, and register it so that Registry.Lookup returns the same controller for the given zone.

requirements.title: UDP Registry.Dial returns ErrAlreadyExists on duplicate zone
requirements.text: A second Registry.Dial call for the same zone shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrAlreadyExists and shall not replace the existing registration.

requirements.title: UDP Registry.Lookup returns ErrNotFound for unregistered zone
requirements.text: Registry.Lookup for a zone that has not been registered shall return a non-nil error wrapping ErrNotFound.

requirements.title: mDNS Transport delivers packets reliably on loopback
requirements.text: The mDNS Transport abstraction shall deliver any packet written by one endpoint to the other endpoint without loss or corruption on loopback interfaces used in testing.

requirements.title: Announcer.Announce emits a well-formed DNS-SD advertisement packet
requirements.text: Announcer.Announce shall write a DNS response packet containing at least one PTR record for _rcp._udp.local. onto the transport. The packet shall be at least 12 bytes.

requirements.title: Browser discovers a zone controller announced on the same bus
requirements.text: A Browser.Query call shall invoke the Handler callback with the correct Zone value when an Announcer on the same transport calls Announce.

requirements.title: Browser reports correct IP:port for discovered zone controller
requirements.text: The addr string passed to the Handler shall equal the IP:port originally provided to NewAnnouncer.

requirements.title: Browser stops reading when context is cancelled
requirements.text: Browser.Query shall return when the provided context is cancelled, without closing the underlying Transport.

requirements.title: Browser ignores non-response DNS packets
requirements.text: Browser.Query shall not invoke the Handler for packets that are DNS queries (QR bit = 0), avoiding false discovery from loopback query echoes.

requirements.title: Browser discovers multiple zone controllers from separate announcements
requirements.text: When N distinct Announcers send announcements onto the same transport, Browser.Query shall invoke the Handler N times with distinct Zone values.

requirements.title: Announcer.Close is idempotent
requirements.text: Calling Announcer.Close more than once shall not return an error on the second or subsequent calls.

requirements.title: TLS Controller Send/Response round-trip succeeds over mTLS
requirements.text: A Controller connected to a ZoneServer with mutual TLS shall successfully Send a Command and receive the corresponding Response with StatusOK.

requirements.title: TLS channel enforces mutual TLS authentication
requirements.text: Connections from clients that do not present a certificate signed by the server's trusted CA shall be rejected during the TLS handshake.

requirements.title: TLS server-side command handler is invoked for each Command
requirements.text: When a ZoneServer handler is installed via SetHandler, it shall be called for every Command received and its returned Response shall be sent to the client.

requirements.title: TLS command payload survives the stream framing round-trip
requirements.text: Payload bytes in a Command shall be identical to the bytes received in the corresponding Response payload echo, byte-for-byte, after passing through TLS framing.

requirements.title: TLS Controller.Send returns ErrZoneMismatch for wrong zone
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return ErrZoneMismatch when cmd.Zone does not equal the Controller's zone, consistent with REQ-CTRL-025.

requirements.title: TLS Controller.Send returns ErrTimeout on cancelled context
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return ErrTimeout when the provided context is cancelled before the send completes.

requirements.title: TLS Controller.Send returns ErrClosed after Close
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return ErrClosed when called after Controller.Close has been called.

requirements.title: TLS Subscribe receives Status published by ZoneServer
requirements.text: A Controller that has called Subscribe shall receive Status frames on the returned channel when ZoneServer.Publish is called.

requirements.title: TLS Subscribe channel closes on context cancellation
requirements.text: The Status channel returned by Subscribe shall be closed when the subscriber's context is cancelled.

requirements.title: TLS minimum protocol version is TLS 1.3
requirements.text: Both ZoneServer and Controller shall negotiate TLS 1.3 or higher. Connections attempting to negotiate TLS 1.2 or lower shall be rejected.

requirements.title: Shmem Controller.Send delivers commands with StatusOK via shared bus
requirements.text: A Controller.Send over a shmem Bus shall receive a Response with StatusOK when no handler is installed, without serialisation or network I/O.

requirements.title: Shmem ZoneServer handler is invoked for each Command
requirements.text: When a handler is installed via ZoneServer.SetHandler, it shall be called for every Command and its Response shall be delivered to the sender.

requirements.title: Shmem Controller.Send copies payload before delivery
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall copy the Command payload into a pooled buffer before dispatching so that mutation of the original slice after Send does not affect the bytes seen by the handler.

requirements.title: Shmem Controller.Send returns ErrZoneMismatch for wrong zone
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return ErrZoneMismatch when cmd.Zone does not match the bus zone, consistent with REQ-CTRL-025.

requirements.title: Shmem Controller.Send returns ErrTimeout on cancelled context
requirements.text: Controller.Send shall return ErrTimeout when the provided context is cancelled before the command is dispatched.

requirements.title: Shmem Subscribe receives Status published by ZoneServer
requirements.text: A Controller.Subscribe channel shall receive a Status frame each time ZoneServer.Publish is called while the subscription is active.

requirements.title: Shmem Subscribe channel closes on context cancellation
requirements.text: The Status channel returned by Subscribe shall be closed when the subscriber's context is cancelled.

requirements.title: Shmem Controller.Send is safe under concurrent callers
requirements.text: Multiple goroutines calling Controller.Send concurrently shall each receive a correct Response without data races or deadlocks.
